codeReview.summary: Reviewed the fix commit, handoff, transcript skeleton, and current onchain implementation. The feature contract is now satisfied: Ethereum pool trades and pool/token OHLCV use live The Graph swap data when available, degrade to fixture data when unavailable, OHLCV aggregation preserves the required invariants, and Ethereum token detail/info pricing now generalizes beyond the old hardcoded token mapping via pool-derived resolution plus live DeFiLlama price lookup.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The onchain-api-worker skill still treats test-first work as mandatory even for explicit commit-only follow-up sessions where implementation already exists in the working tree and the worker is only asked to verify and create the missing clean commit.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/whoami/dev/openGecko/.factory/skills/onchain-api-worker/SKILL.md` step 4 requires writing failing tests first, while the transcript skeleton for worker session `fdaeb825-db1b-4001-87e1-2a1b2624163d` shows a verification-and-commit session and the handoff marks this as a justified procedure deviation.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the prior failed review, mission metadata, worker transcript skeleton, handoff, commit `8e3fc98`, and the current `src/modules/onchain.ts` and test coverage. The previously blocking gap is fixed: `fetchLiveSimpleTokenPrice` now resolves coin context from discoverable token pools, so non-hardcoded Ethereum token detail/info routes can surface live DeFiLlama pricing while preserving seeded fallback behavior. The diff and tests also provide adequate evidence for live/fallback pool trades and OHLCV behavior, including swap-derived candle invariants and fixture-backed degraded mode when The Graph is unavailable.
